title: Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud at Google Cloud Next 2026: 15 Regions, New Restore Capabilities, and Gemini Session
summary: At Google Cloud Next 2026 (April 22–24, Las Vegas), Oracle announced Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud now spans 15 regions (20 sites) globally — adding 7 new regions across Asia Pacific, Europe, North America, and Latin America. New restore capabilities for Autonomous AI Database give users greater control to recover from data loss or corruption with reduced operational risk. The session 'Talk to your data with Gemini Enterprise' previews an upcoming integration connecting Google Gemini with Oracle AI Database for enterprise data querying.

title: Stargate Michigan: OpenAI and Oracle Sign Union Labor Agreement — 2,500 AI Infrastructure Construction Jobs
summary: On April 20, at NABTU's Legislative Conference in Washington D.C., OpenAI, Oracle, and Related Digital signed a union labor agreement to build the Stargate Michigan data center — the first Stargate site committed to union-built construction under an agreement with NABTU, expected to create 2,500 skilled trades jobs. The flagship Abilene, Texas campus is already live with 1.2 GW of Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, with construction underway across additional US states. Total program investment exceeds $400 billion with planned capacity approaching 7 GW.

title: Oracle Health Achieves CMS Aligned Network Status and Launches 'Kill the Clipboard' Digital Check-In
summary: On April 20, Oracle Health announced it achieved CMS Aligned Network status and launched a solution supporting CMS's 'Kill the Clipboard' initiative. Leveraging CLEAR, the solution enables patients to digitally verify their identity and share health records at check-in — eliminating redundant paperwork and manual data entry. The announcement reinforces Oracle Health's leadership in healthcare interoperability and its commitment to reducing administrative burden in clinical settings.

title: Oracle at Google Cloud Next 2026: Gemini and Oracle AI Database — New AI Integration for Enterprise Data
summary: At Google Cloud Next 2026, Oracle and Google are presenting a joint AI strategy: Oracle AI Database@Google Cloud enables querying enterprise Oracle data through Google Gemini in the 'Talk to your data with Gemini Enterprise' session. Oracle APEX developers will be able to integrate Gemini with Oracle AI Database without moving data outside a secured perimeter. The Oracle-Google Cloud partnership now spans 15 regions, with Oracle Database deployed natively in Google Cloud data centers as a jointly managed service.

title: Oracle Critical Patch Update April 2026: 483 New Security Patches for Database, ORDS, and GoldenGate
summary: On April 21, Oracle released its quarterly Critical Patch Update (CPU) for April 2026 — 483 new security patches. Oracle Database received 8 patches, 4 of which are remotely exploitable without authentication; affected versions include 19c (19.3–19.30), 21c (21.3–21.21), and AI Database 26ai (23.4–23.26.1). Oracle REST Data Services: 2 patches (both remote, CVSS 7.5). GoldenGate: 10 patches, 7 remote. Oracle strongly recommends applying patches as soon as possible. Quarterly CPUs represent a fundamental difference from legacy systems like 1C/BAS, which receive no centralized security patches after being banned.

title: Oracle APEX April 2026 Newsletter: Agentic AI with Oracle AI Database and the Acordia Enterprise Case
summary: The April 2026 Oracle APEX Newsletter features a deep dive on Agentic AI with Oracle AI Database — how 26ai and APEX enable agentic automation for business applications without external AI infrastructure. The exclusive Acordia case study (founder Vincent Lockyer) shows an enterprise data lifecycle management solution built on Oracle APEX and OCI. Also included: new ACE community blog picks and upcoming spring events — APEX Connect 2026 at Heide Park (May 18) and the final North America Bootcamp stops.

title: OCI Database Migration Service Gets Assessment Feature — Automated Readiness Check Before Migration
summary: OCI Database Migration Service introduced the Assessment feature: automated analysis of an existing database, recommendation of the optimal migration method, and schema and user readiness validation. The feature reduces the risk of unexpected incompatibilities during migration and enables a technical audit before the project begins. For organizations planning a move from legacy ERP to Oracle, the automated assessment provides a practical first step without engaging consultants at the initial stage.

title: Oracle Database Cloud BYOL: Move Existing Oracle Licenses to OCI Without Repurchasing
summary: Oracle published an updated guide on BYOL (Bring Your Own License) strategy for Oracle Database Cloud on OCI. Customers with existing Oracle Database EE or SE2 licenses can bring them to OCI at no additional licensing cost using Authorized Cloud Environments. This significantly reduces migration costs for enterprises that already have on-premises Oracle Database licenses. For organizations that have already invested in Oracle Database while migrating away from legacy ERP, BYOL removes a key financial barrier to moving workloads to OCI.

title: APEX AI Interactive Reports: Query Your Report in Natural Language — Millions of Existing IRs Become AI-Ready in APEX 26.1
summary: Oracle published 'Talk to Your Data, Trust the Result. Introducing APEX AI Interactive Reports.' The key APEX 26.1 advantage: any existing Interactive Report becomes AI-enabled after a platform upgrade — no application code changes required. An LLM converts natural language questions into SQL filters and aggregates that users can see and edit directly for full transparency. A built-in chat mode is also available within the IR component. For enterprise analytics teams on Oracle APEX, this means instant AI enrichment of all existing reports without rebuilding them.

title: Oracle AI Database Private Agent Factory: No-Code Platform for Enterprise AI Agents on Corporate Data
summary: On March 24, Oracle introduced Private Agent Factory — a no-code component of Oracle AI Database 26ai for building and deploying trusted AI agents. Agents are built on private corporate data with full audit trail and security controls, without extracting data externally. The platform supports dynamic agentic workflows, external REST API integration, and MCP server connections. Finished agents deploy as portable containers and integrate with Oracle AI Agent Memory — a persistent agent memory layer built into the database.

title: Oracle AI Agent Memory: Unified Persistent Agent Memory Built Directly Into the Database
summary: Oracle introduced the Unified Memory Core — a built-in persistent memory engine for AI agents in Oracle AI Database 26ai. Rather than maintaining separate graph, document, and vector stores, a single unified layer handles all types of agent memory: short-term (session context), long-term (facts and preferences), and semantic (vector associations). AI Agent Memory integrates with Private Agent Factory and enables agents to 'remember' across sessions without external RAG infrastructure. Expected availability: 2026.

title: Oracle EBS 12.2 Certified for Oracle AI Database 26ai on On-Premises Linux x86-64
summary: On April 7, Oracle confirmed certification of Oracle E-Business Suite Release 12.2 for use with Oracle AI Database 26ai (RU 23.26.1) on on-premises Linux x86-64. EBS 12.2 customers on database 19c get a direct upgrade path to 26ai via the AutoUpgrade utility — no application-layer changes required. Alongside certification, Oracle introduced the EDBPC (EBS Database Parameter Checker) utility to validate database parameters before migration. For enterprises planning a move to Oracle AI Database, this removes a key technical barrier.

title: OCI: Oracle Container Engine Supports Kubernetes 1.35 'Timbernetes' — In-Place Pod Resource Updates
summary: Oracle Container Engine for Kubernetes (OKE) added support for Kubernetes 1.35, codenamed 'Timbernetes.' Key highlights: in-place CPU/memory resource updates for Pods without restarting — significant for stateful and restart-sensitive workloads; improved scheduling and security foundations. Clusters on Oracle Linux 7 must migrate to OL8+ since Kubernetes 1.35 requires cgroup v2. For enterprise teams building Oracle APEX applications with Kubernetes orchestration, this version enables finer-grained resource tuning without service interruptions.

title: Oracle Database@AWS Now Available in 12 Global Regions: Frankfurt, Tokyo, Ohio and More
summary: Oracle Database@AWS expanded to 12 AWS regions worldwide. New regions include Frankfurt (EU-Central-1), Tokyo (AP-Northeast-1), Ohio (US-East-2), London (EU-West-2), Mumbai (AP-South-1), Hyderabad (AP-South-2), and Seoul (AP-Northeast-2). The service lets customers run Oracle Exadata and Oracle RAC natively inside AWS — without moving data to OCI. European and Asia-Pacific organizations with in-region data residency requirements now have full support. For businesses migrating from legacy ERP to Oracle, this eliminates the 'we're already on AWS' objection.

title: OCI Cache Valkey 8.1: New Release with JSON Module and Vector Similarity Search
summary: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Cache updated to Valkey 8.1. The new release includes a native JSON module, valkey-search for high-performance vector similarity search, improved I/O threading (up to 3.5x faster for KEYS operations and replication), an optimized hash table with reduced per-key memory usage, and iterator prefetching. For Oracle APEX applications using semantic search and RAG scenarios, Valkey 8.1 enables vector search directly in the cache layer — no additional AI infrastructure required.

title: Oracle + Bloom Energy: 2.8 GW Fuel Cell Deal for OCI AI Data Centers — Grid-Independent Cloud Power
summary: On April 14, Bloom Energy and Oracle announced an expanded strategic partnership: Oracle will procure up to 2.8 gigawatts of solid oxide fuel cell (SOFC) systems to power its AI data centers in the US. An initial 1.2 GW is already contracted and being deployed. Bloom delivered the first system in 55 days — 35 days ahead of the expected 90-day schedule. The fuel cells operate independently of the electrical grid and support high-density AI workloads. Market reaction: Bloom Energy stock surged 22%.

title: APEX Alpe Adria 2026: Oracle APEX Community Conference Held in Ljubljana
summary: On April 17, APEX Alpe Adria 2026 took place at Four Points by Sheraton Ljubljana — the annual Oracle APEX community conference organized by Slovenia, Croatia, and Austria. Key topics: APEXlang and the .apx format for DevOps-native development, APEX 26.1 AI Copilot, next-generation Interactive Reports, and Map Regions. The event reinforces growing Central European interest in Oracle APEX and the strengthening ecosystem around APEX 26.1 as an enterprise low-code platform.

title: Oracle and AWS Expand Multicloud Networking: Private Managed Connection Between OCI and AWS
summary: On April 16, Oracle and AWS announced expanded multicloud networking collaboration. Enterprise customers will gain a fast, private, managed connection between OCI and AWS — without separate network providers or physical infrastructure. Built on Oracle Interconnect and AWS Interconnect, supporting full-stack and split-stack multicloud deployments. First availability: AWS US East (N. Virginia). OCI is already interconnected with 26 partner cloud regions.

title: Oracle APEX 26.1: AI in Interactive Reports, Pattern Pages & New Universal Theme — Community Review
summary: The Into The Oracle Verse blog published a detailed review of APEX 26.1 innovations. AI in Interactive Reports lets users query data tables in natural language — the system auto-applies filters, builds charts, and responds in a chat interface. Pattern Pages simplify UI pattern reuse across large teams. The new Universal Theme adds advanced mobile features. APEXlang AI Copilot generates complete applications from a plain-text description.

title: Oracle AI Database 26ai: Platinum and Diamond Availability Tiers for Mission-Critical Workloads
summary: On April 9, Oracle announced new availability tiers for Oracle AI Database 26ai on Exadata. Platinum tier delivers failover in under 30 seconds for Active Data Guard and RAC — no application changes required. Diamond tier combines Exadata, RAC, Active Data Guard, and Zero Data Loss Recovery for stock-exchange-level availability, with no manual intervention needed. Up to 4x faster failover for large, complex deployments — enterprise-grade mission-critical availability without specialized in-house expertise.

title: Oracle Data Deep Dive NYC: AI Database as the Foundation for Agentic AI — A New Architectural Paradigm
summary: On April 15, Oracle held Oracle Data Deep Dive NYC in New York — an event focused on its AI database convergence strategy for enterprise agentic AI. Oracle bets on a unified engine approach: vector search, graph, JSON, relational, and agent memory — without purpose-built AI silos. Exadata Exascale uses Raft replication for sub-3-second zero-data-loss failover designed specifically for agentic workloads. Oracle challenges the 'orchestration above data' paradigm: agent logic should execute as close to the data as possible, not above fragmented data stores.

title: Oracle EPM April 2026 (26.04): Fusion AI Planning Agent — From Quarterly Cycles to Event-Driven FP&A
summary: Oracle's April 2026 Fusion Cloud EPM update (26.04) integrates Fusion AI Agents into financial planning workflows. The Planning Agent shifts FP&A teams from quarterly cycles to event-driven planning: real-time variance and trend analysis via natural language, what-if simulations on Fusion ERP/EPM data, and automated narrative reporting. New REST APIs enable programmatic agentic AI interaction with EPM Cloud. Predictive Cash Forecasting — previously available only in beta — is now open to all customers.

title: Qualys VMDR & TotalCloud Now on Oracle Cloud Marketplace: One-Click OCI Security
summary: On April 15, Qualys announced availability of VMDR (Vulnerability Management, Detection and Response) and TotalCloud CNAPP on the Oracle Cloud Marketplace. The solution delivers unified visibility across OCI cloud, containers, workloads, and applications with risk-based vulnerability prioritization. One-click provisioning via native OCI integration. Customers can use existing OCI committed spend — no separate procurement process required.

title: OCI Java Management Service: JDK 26 Support — Oracle First Public Cloud Provider
summary: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Java Management Service added support for JDK 26, released March 17, 2026. OCI became the first public cloud provider to offer Oracle JDK 26 — featuring AI integration, new security tooling, and performance improvements. Oracle also announced the Java Verified Portfolio (JVP) — a curated set of Oracle-supported tools, frameworks, and libraries, free for Java SE subscribers and OCI customers running Java workloads on OCI.

title: Oracle Financial Services Extends Agentic AI Platform to Corporate Banking: Agents for Loans, Guarantees & SCF
summary: At the Oracle Financial Services Summit on April 14, Oracle expanded its agentic AI platform to corporate banking. New pre-built AI agents cover the full cycle: Loan Data Extraction Agent processes lengthy credit agreements, Financial Data Validation Agent verifies financial inputs, Application Validator Agent handles bank guarantee applications, and SCF Program Creation Agent launches supply chain finance programs. Built with human-in-the-loop governance. Oracle plans 100+ corporate and retail banking agents within 12 months.

title: Oracle Primavera Unifier Gets AI Agents for Capital Project Management — Oracle Stock +6.5%
summary: On April 14, Oracle announced an AI overhaul for Primavera Unifier, its flagship capital project management platform. New capabilities: AI workflow summaries, no-code/low-code process automation via Oracle Integration, real-time adapters and triggers, improved reporting dashboards, and audit-ready compliance. Oracle Integration gives AI agents secure, fully auditable access to enterprise data. Markets responded with Oracle stock surging 6.5% on April 14, 2026.

title: Oracle AI World Tour Singapore: SMRT Deploys OCI Enterprise AI for Rail Maintenance Intelligence
summary: At Oracle AI World Tour Singapore on April 14, SMRT — Singapore's leading public transportation provider — announced a pilot using OCI Enterprise AI and Oracle Autonomous AI Database. The JARVIS platform (built by STRIDES Technologies) unifies operational data from multiple systems and applies AI for rail infrastructure condition analysis via a generative AI chatbot interface. The case demonstrates Oracle AI applicability in mission-critical industrial infrastructure.

title: Oracle Financial Crime & Compliance: New AI Agents for Suspicious Transaction Detection and Compliance
summary: On April 9, Oracle announced enhancements to the Oracle FCCM (Financial Crime and Compliance Management) platform with Lucinity technology and new AI agents. Agents automate detection of suspicious financial transactions, compliance verification, and transaction monitoring. For banks and financial institutions migrating from legacy systems, Oracle now offers a fully AI-native financial compliance infrastructure.

title: OCI Enterprise AI Goes GA: Oracle Unifies AI Models, Agents, and Governance in One Platform
summary: On March 26, Oracle announced the general availability of OCI Enterprise AI — a platform combining OCI Enterprise AI Models (frontier model access), OCI Enterprise AI Agents (build and deploy agentic workloads using managed tools and open-source frameworks), and OCI Enterprise AI Governance (enterprise-grade security and compliance). OpenAI Responses API compatibility lets teams reuse existing code patterns. On April 15, Oracle hosts an introductory webinar.

title: OCI Compute: AMD Instinct MI355X GPUs — Oracle First Public Hyperscaler
summary: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure became the first public cloud provider to offer AMD Instinct MI355X GPUs. OCI Supercluster scales to 131,072 GPUs. Each MI355X carries 288 GB of HBM3E memory at 8 TB/s bandwidth — nearly 3x more powerful than the previous generation. In Q3 2026, Oracle and AMD plan to deploy an AMD Instinct MI450 Series supercluster with 50,000 GPUs.

title: Ukraine Bill 13505: Up to 2% Revenue Fine for Failing to Migrate from 1C and BAS
summary: Ukraine's draft law No. 13505 includes a direct financial penalty: a fine of up to 2% of annual revenue for companies that do not complete migration by the set deadlines. Banks and investors already have grounds to refuse financing to companies whose policies reflect continued use of 'hostile software.' Over 300,000 Ukrainian companies are estimated to still run on 1C or BAS.

title: APEXlang in APEX 26.1: ACE Community Explains the DevOps Approach to Low-Code
summary: The Oracle ACE Program published its April 2026 APEXlang must-read roundup. MaxAPEX's blog breaks down the .apx format in depth: a human-readable YAML/JSON for building complete APEX applications with git version control and CI/CD support. APEXlang AI Copilot enables generating and editing applications in natural language. Community consensus: APEX 26.1 is a qualitative leap in DevOps-native low-code development.

title: APEX Alpe Adria 2026: Oracle APEX Conference in Ljubljana, April 16–17
summary: The Oracle APEX community conference organized by Slovenia, Croatia, and Austria takes place April 16–17 at Four Points by Sheraton Ljubljana. Agenda covers APEXlang, new APEX 26.1 features, AI Copilot, and DevOps integration. Part of the growing wave of APEX community events in spring 2026.

title: OCI Autonomous AI Database: Critical Wallet Update Required by April 15
summary: Oracle warns: DigiCert is revoking trust in G1 root certificates on April 15, 2026. Users of Oracle Autonomous AI Database Serverless and Essbase on OCI Marketplace must download a new wallet (issued after January 28, 2026) before the deadline to avoid losing database connectivity.

title: Oracle APEX Bootcamps North America: Free Tour Covering APEX 26.1 & APEXlang
summary: Oracle is running a series of free one-day APEX Bootcamps from April 1 to May 7 across five cities in the US, Canada, and Mexico (Boston, Reston, Minneapolis, Chicago, Guadalajara). Attendees get access to OCI Gen AI and explore APEXlang and the APEX 26.1 approach to AI-driven development.

title: APEX Connect 2026: 12th Annual Oracle APEX Conference at Heide Park Soltau
summary: The 12th edition of APEX Connect takes place May 18, 2026, at Heide Park Soltau, Germany. Organized by DOAG, the leading Oracle community in the DACH region. Topics: APEXlang, AI in APEX, next-generation Interactive Reports, Map Regions, Dynamic Actions, and PL/SQL APIs.

title: Fusion Agentic Applications for HR: 8 AI Agents in Oracle HCM
summary: Oracle announced 8 new AI-agent applications within Oracle Fusion Cloud HCM. Career Advancement Command Center, Contract Compliance Workspace and others automate HR processes — career mobility, contract compliance, and workforce decisions — inside a unified enterprise platform without third-party integrations.

title: Fusion Agentic Applications for Finance & SCM: 12 AI Agents for ERP and Supply Chains
summary: Oracle introduced 12 new AI-agent applications across Oracle Fusion Cloud ERP and SCM. Claims Settlement Workspace accelerates claim resolution and improves financial accuracy. Agents automate financial operations, supply chain management, and manufacturing within a unified agentic ERP stack.

title: OCI Generative AI: Import NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super 120B and Qwen 3 Embedding Models
summary: OCI Generative AI added support for importing open-weights models: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super 120B (high-performance reasoning and instruction following) and Qwen 3 Embedding (text embedding and reranking for enterprise RAG scenarios). Models are available for deployment within a customer's own OCI tenancy.

title: OCI Cache: Valkey 8 with Native JSON and Vector Similarity Search
summary: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure Cache announced support for Valkey 8 with improved I/O threading (up to 3.5x faster for KEYS operations and replication), native JSON module, and Valkey-search for high-performance vector similarity search. New capabilities open semantic search scenarios in cache without additional infrastructure.
